_comment: EventBridge rules that drive real-time discovery off CloudTrail. Two tiers: (1) born-public/exposed events we parse straight into a scannable Asset, and (2) became-public / signal-only events that need an L2 correlation worker (read-only Describe*) to resolve the actual public target before scanning. CloudTrail management events must be enabled in the account/org; Route53 + CloudFront are global services whose events land in us-east-1.

tier1-directly-scannable._doc: Parsed by discovery/cloudtrail.py into an Asset and enqueued immediately.

tier2-needs-correlation._doc: Signal-only 'became public' events. The target isn't in the event (an open SG/ECS task maps to N resources / an async-assigned IP), so these go to an L2 correlation worker that calls EC2 DescribeNetworkInterfaces / DescribeInstances (read-only) to resolve the actual public IP(s), then enqueues Assets. Documented here; the prototype implements tier 1.

_notes: errorCode exists:false filters out failed API calls (no asset was actually created).
AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress is only interesting when the rule opens 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 — filter in the correlation worker, then resolve which ENIs/instances use that SG.

EKS/ECS ingress: a k8s Ingress or Service type=LoadBalancer surfaces here as a CreateLoadBalancer event (the AWS Load Balancer Controller calls ELB), so it is ALREADY covered by tier 1. For k8s-native visibility, also ship EKS control-plane *audit* logs to CloudWatch and run a subscription filter on Ingress/Service objects.

Non-AWS DNS (records in Cloudflare/GoDaddy pointing at AWS) is invisible to CloudTrail — cover it with the L3 external layer: Certificate Transparency (crt.sh) new-cert monitoring + periodic subdomain enumeration.
For org-wide coverage, deploy on the org event bus or per-account via StackSets. A nightly AWS Config / Resource Groups Tagging API reconciliation backstops anything missed.
